title: WOLFF-KISHNER REDUCTION
summary: Overview page for the Wolff-Kishner reduction describing hydrazone- or semicarbazone-based deoxygenation of aldehydes and ketones to hydrocarbons under strongly basic, high-temperature conditions, including Huang-Minlon, Cram, and related modifications.
notes: Emphasizes hydrazone formation, base-promoted extrusion of nitrogen through dimide-like intermediates, and operational variants that lower temperature or shorten reaction time.

title: WOLFF-KISHNER REDUCTION
summary: Application page for the Wolff-Kishner reduction featuring ketone deoxygenation in the syntheses of methyl kaurene and trachylobane derivatives, aspidospermidine, dysidiolide, and steroidal frameworks under classical or modified hydrazone reduction conditions.
notes: Examples show late-stage carbonyl removal in densely functionalized polycycles, including Myers-type one-pot variants and combinations with lactam reduction or protecting-group manipulations.

title: WURTZ COUPLING
summary: Overview page for the Wurtz coupling describing sodium- or metal-mediated coupling of alkyl, allylic, benzylic, or aryl halides to form new carbon-carbon bonds, including homocoupling, cross-coupling, and Wurtz-Fittig variants.
notes: Covers classical sodium-promoted dimerization, limits from elimination and rearrangement, modified protocols using solubilizing additives such as tetraphenylethylene, and mechanistic proposals involving organosodium or radical pathways.

title: WURTZ COUPLING
summary: Application page for the Wurtz coupling highlighting formation of steroid dimers, macrocycles such as garugamblin-1 and marchantin I, and cyclobutanes from dibromide precursors under classical or modified sodium-mediated coupling conditions.
notes: Examples emphasize intramolecular reductive coupling for ring closure and macrocyclization, as well as TPE-assisted modified Wurtz protocols.

title: YAMAGUCHI MACROLACTONIZATION
summary: Overview page for the Yamaguchi macrolactonization describing conversion of hydroxy acids into medium- and large-ring lactones through mixed anhydride formation with 2,4,6-trichlorobenzoyl chloride and high-dilution DMAP-promoted cyclization.
notes: Highlights mixed anhydride generation, slow addition under high dilution, and DMAP-mediated intramolecular acyl transfer as the basis for efficient macrocycle formation.

title: YAMAGUCHI MACROLACTONIZATION
summary: Application page for the Yamaguchi macrolactonization featuring macrolactin A, oleandolide, and laulimalide syntheses, where high-dilution mixed-anhydride cyclization efficiently closes 14- to 24-membered lactones in advanced polyketide settings.
notes: Examples show standard and modified Yamaguchi protocols, conformational effects on ring closure, and downstream manipulations such as Lindlar reduction, Sharpless epoxidation, and PMB deprotection.
